brief_justification: Arrives at exact correct answer (B) with valid SCP hierarchy reasoning; goes one level beyond surface by explaining AND-gate evaluation logic and why SCPs cap rather than grant permissions; no explicit self-monitoring or bias-naming, only implicit recheck via option elimination; verifies by falsifying all three wrong options with specific reasons; presents conclusion confidently without structured uncertainty analysis or confidence levels; systematically evaluates all three alternatives with specific rejection reasons; every conclusion traces to an explicit rule and the hierarchy table is reproducible; avoids the information-asymmetry anti-pattern (does not assume IAM overrides SCP) but does not explicitly name the gate; avoids post-hoc rationalization and single-hypothesis lock but reasoning is predominantly deductive; response is linear despite the topology calling for per-agent zone mapping and M-node self-observation loops.
